subject: mathematics

core_competencies.justification: The chapter's entire pedagogical spine is understanding and visualising addition and subtraction with regrouping, first through Dienes-block and token models (showing how 10 Ones regroup to 1 Ten and 10 Tens to 1 Hundred), and then applying these operations to solve a wide range of daily-life problems — computing shopping bills, finding change returned to customers, comparing how many children bought different snacks, and tracking passengers across train stations.

The H-T-O (Hundreds-Tens-Ones) place-value structure is made explicit throughout the Add Up and Subtract It sections: the column algorithm is presented with H, T, O column headers, regrouping is explained as an exchange between place-value positions, and the Cost–Paid–Balance tasks require students to compare 2- and 3-digit numbers in order to determine the smaller from the larger.

adjunct_competencies.justification: The chapter includes three puzzle types that go beyond routine computation: the Magic Triangle (distributing 1–6 across triangle nodes to achieve target side-sums of 9 and 10, and exploring other achievable sums), the Number Pair Hunt (identifying adjacent grid pairs with the greatest/smallest sum or difference), and the Missing Digits challenge (using reverse-reasoning to fill blanks in addition and subtraction sums).

Estimation prompts appear before every major worked computation (teachers on the trip, children on the trip, money spent on pusaw, change from the orange purchase), explicitly asking students to form a mental estimate before applying the paper-pencil algorithm. A dedicated quick-strategy task requires students to solve six expressions such as 326+25 and 410−12 using flexible mental methods, with a speech-bubble model illustrating the strategy.

The shopping section poses an open combination task — 'Their mother buys things for ₹163; what might she have bought? There is more than one possibility' — requiring students to systematically list additive combinations of fruit and vegetable quantities from the price list that satisfy the target sum, an introductory enumeration of possibilities under a constraint.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to add and subtract whole numbers up to three digits by moving from concrete place-value models (Dienes blocks and tokens) to the column algorithm with regrouping, and applying both operations flexibly across shopping, travel, and puzzle contexts.
